channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Follow live: France, Morocco scoreless as Mbappé d...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: null

item.title: Sources: Pulisic has microfracture, bone bruise
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: USMNT and AC Milan attacker Christian Pulisic sustained a bone bruise and microfracture in his lower leg and will be sidelined for several weeks, U.S. Soccer confirmed on Thursday.

item.title: Haaland says Norway's WC chances 'still really low...
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Norway striker Erling Haaland has said that his side's chances of winning the World Cup are "really low" -- and that all the pressure is on England ahead of their World Cup quarterfinal in Miami.

item.title: ⚽ World Cup Golden Boot tracker: Messi leads
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: We're into the quarterfinals at the 2026 FIFA World Cup, and the race for the Golden Boot -- given to the tournament's top goal scorer -- is red-hot.

item.title: Source: Barça bid for Dortmund's Karim Adeyemi
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Barcelona have made an offer for Borussia Dortmund's Karim Adeyemi as they continue to build their attack for next season, a source told ESPN.

item.title: Eriksen to rehab in Denmark after collapse
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: German club VfL Wolfsburg said Denmark star Christian Eriksen will start an individual rehabilitation program in his native country after another on-field collapse last month.

item.title: England's Quansah gets 2-game ban for Ro16 red
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: England fullback Jarell Quansah has been suspended for two matches after receiving a red card in his side's World Cup round-of-16 win over Mexico.

item.title: Sources: Utd, Chelsea agree $67M deal for Santos
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Manchester United have agreed a deal with Chelsea for midfielder Andrey Santos, sources have told ESPN.

item.title: U.S.'s Campbell joins Elversberg from Dortmund
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: American forward Cole Campbell is joining Bundesliga newcomer Elversberg from Borussia Dortmund.

item.title: Cherundolo to coach U.S. U-23s at '28 Olympics
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The U.S. Soccer Federation named former United States international and MLS Cup-winning coach Steve Cherundolo to be the manager of the U.S. U-23 national team that will represent the country at the 2028 Summer Olympics.

item.title: Highlights: England thrash India AGAIN to seal landmark series victory
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The best of the action from the fourth T20I in a sunny Bristol as England demolished India for the second time this week to secure a first-ever series win over their opponents in the format.

item.title: Iran says projectile hit military base near Bushehr
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Iranian state media reported Thursday that a US-Israeli projectile hit military headquarters on the outskirts of Bushehr, hours after clashes between the United States and Iran flared. The post Iran says projectile hit military base near Bushehr appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: SK Hynix Is Said to Price US Share Offering at $149 Apiece
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. priced its US listing of American depositary receipts at $149 each, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified as the information isn’t public.

item.title: Meet the new group of stocks powering the S&P 500 higher
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: A violent rotation is taking place under the hood of the stock market ahead of second-quarter earnings.

item.title: Spurs make offer for Kolo Muani - Friday gossip
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Tottenham make an offer for Randal Kolo Muani but Juventus remain favourites to sign the forward, Arsenal are ready to finalise a deal for Christos Tzolis, Marcus Rashford's future at Barcelona could be in doubt, plus more.

item.title: First Lady commissions remodeled Abuja City Gate, praises Wike as Renewed Hope agent
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: First Lady of Oluremi Tinubu has officially commissioned the remodeled Abuja City Gate, describing the landmark as a symbol of national unity. The post First Lady commissions remodeled Abuja City Gate, praises Wike as Renewed Hope agent appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: 2027: Akpabio Urges Tinubu Support Group To Mobilise For Unity
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The President of the Senate, Senator Godswill Akpabio, has charged the National Executives and members of the President Bola Ahmed Tinubu, PBAT, Door-to-Door Movement to promote national unity while seeking support for President Tinubu’s re-election in 2027. Akpabio gave the charge on Thursday durin

item.title: Chipmakers Boost Stocks as Oil Declines | Closing Bell
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Lisa Mateo. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Transfer: Ugochukwu set to join Osimhen at Galatasaray
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Burnley midfielder, Lesley Ugochukwu, is close to joining Galatasaray this summer. The two clubs have been locked in negotiations and a deal is believed to be close. This was revealed by football transfer expert, Fabrizio Romano, on X. Romano wrote: “Galatasaray are confident to get Lesley Ugo

item.title: Tinubu meets service chiefs over worsening insecurity
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Thursday held a closed-door meeting with the nation's security chiefs at the The post Tinubu meets service chiefs over worsening insecurity appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Bandits Using Abducted Oyo Pupils To Bargain For Commanders’ Release – Defence Minister
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: BY ANDREW OJIEZEL, Lagos The Minister of Defence, Christopher Musa, has revealed that the bandits who abducted 39 pupils and seven teachers from schools in Oyo State are using the victims as bargaining chips to demand the release of their commanders currently in military custody. The pupils and teac

item.title: State Police: Mbah Presses For Financial Autonomy, Investment In Technology
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State has called for financial autonomy and investment in modern security infrastructure for state police services when created, stressing the need for any legal framework for state police to take into account the need for national standards, national support, local capa

item.title: First-Ever FIFA Final Halftime Show: Bieber, BTS, Madonna, Burna Boy And Shakira Make World Cup History
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Global music icons Justin Bieber, BTS, Madonna and Shakira are set to share one stage for the first-ever halftime show in FIFA World Cup final history. The landmark performance will headline the 2026 FIFA World Cup final on July 19 at the New York-New Jersey Stadium, marking the first time FIFA has

item.title: Brook, Salt blast England to T20 series win over dismal India
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Harry Brook and Phil Salt's 146-run partnership saw England dish out a nine-wicket trouncing of India in Bristol to cruise to a T20 series victory with a game to spare.

item.title: The ChatGPT browser is already dead
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: OpenAI is already shutting down ChatGPT Atlas, its browser that could do tasks for you on your behalf, less than a year after launching it. Atlas was announced in October, but as part of its wave of news about ChatGPT Work today, the company confirmed that it will be "sunsetting" Atlas and is target

item.title: Caramell Plug Calls Out Anita Joseph Over Cropped Photo, Sparks Social Media Buzz
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Content creator Caramell Plug has publicly called out Nollywood actress Anita Joseph after alleging that the actress edited her out of a photo they took together. Caramel Plug took to social media to express her disappointment after noticing that only Anita Joseph appeared in a version of the pictur

item.title: The floatable, powerful Soundcore Boom 2 speaker is over half off
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Bluetooth speakers with big sound and great features are hard to find for under $100, with most offerings being some variation of the same basic (and often small) design. Thankfully, through July 10th Woot has the Anker Soundcore Boom 2 on sale for $69.99, which is $20 cheaper than the discounted pr

item.title: UNIOSUN records global history, wins prestigious Hult Prize Award
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Despite participating for the first time, UNIOSUN distinguished itself by establishing a vibrant Hult Prize ecosystem that fostered innovation and entrepreneurship across its six campuses.

item.title: Residents Hail Kaduna Road Projects, Seek Lasting Infrastructure Solutions
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The Kaduna Roads Agency assured residents that the roads were being constructed to durable standards. The post Residents Hail Kaduna Road Projects, Seek Lasting Infrastructure Solutions appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother.”

item.title: Tinubu Meets Service Chiefs Behind Closed Doors At State House
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Thursday presided over a closed-door security meeting at the Presidential Villa, Abuja, with the country's top military commanders, intelligence chiefs and security advisers. The post Tinubu Meets Service Chiefs Behind Closed Doors At State House appeared first on Trending N

item.title: 2027: We have no business in opposition – Kogi APC stakeholders
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Prominent stakeholders of the All Progressives Congress, APC, in Kogi East Senatorial District, under the auspices of the Kogi Progressive Stakeholders Forum (KPSF), have declared that they have no business in opposition political parties ahead of the 2027 general elections. The APC stakeholders als

item.title: Laws that centralize police authorization in Nigeria have failed – Deputy Speaker Kalu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Deputy Speaker of House of Representatives, Benjamin Kalu, says obsolete laws cebtralised the authorization of police in Nigeria have failed. Kalu made this remark on Thursday during Arise Television’s Town Hall on State Police as he threw his weight on the creation of state police in the coun

item.title: Polymarket Seeks License to Offer Margin Trading Legally in US
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Polymarket is seeking regulatory approval to offer margin trading in the US, a move that would let users bet on events with less capital upfront and help the prediction market platform attract more sophisticated traders.

item.title: UBEC Unlocks N100bn Trapped Education Funds For States, FCT
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Universal Basic Education Commission(UBEC) has facilitated the mobilisation of over N100bn in previously unaccessed matching grants by states and the Federal Capital Territory(FCT). The Executive Secretary of UBEC, Hajia Aisha Garba, disclosed this on Thursday at a media luncheon with Education

item.title: Landon Donovan says there’s ‘zero chance’ he could have afforded to play youth soccer if he was a kid today
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The U.S. men’s soccer team once again didn’t make it past the Round of 16 in the World Cup — and some say the high cost to play youth soccer in America could be a key reason.

item.title: Mexico seeks prosecutions over migrant deaths in the US
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Mexico will seek state and federal prosecutions in the United States over the deaths of Mexican migrants.

item.title: Senate approves N404bn Police Trust Fund budgets for 2025, 2026
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Senate has approved a total budget of N404.07 billion for the Nigeria Police Trust Fund (NPTF), giving the green light to its 2025 and 2026 expenditure plans aimed at improving policing, security operations and infrastructure nationwide. The approved allocation includes N170.10 billion for the 2

item.title: Speaker Abbas, Kalu task parliamentary correspondents on promoting national unity
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Hon. Benjamin Kalu, on Thursday charged parliamentary correspondents to promote The post Speaker Abbas, Kalu task parliamentary correspondents on promoting national unity appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: UK Labour’s Burnham apologises for response to Israel’s Gaza violence
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: UK's likely next PM Burnham pushes for ceasefire, tougher sanctions over Gaza violence and settlement trade bans.

item.title: NPFL: Falolu close in on Rangers move
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Azeez Falolu is on the verge of securing a move to Rangers for the 2026-27 season, DAILY POST reports. According to reports, Falolu has agreed personal terms with the Flying Antelopes. Rangers tactician Fidelis Ilechukwu is reported to be an admirer of the midfielder. Falolu’s arrival would ad

item.title: Ex-Works Director Backs Concrete Roads, Seeks Higher Funding
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The retiring Director of Highways Construction and Rehabilitation, Engr. Clement Ogbuagu, on Thursday, disclosed that the shift to concrete pavements has improved road quality nationwide. Ogbuagu, who spoke at a valedictory session held in his honour at the Ministry’s headquarters, in Abuja, commend

item.title: Ndume Meets Tinubu Over Insecurity, Advocates Sustained Military Offensive
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The fight against insecurity took centre stage as Senator Ali Ndume met with President Bola Tinubu at the Presidential Villa in Abuja. The post Ndume Meets Tinubu Over Insecurity, Advocates Sustained Military Offensive appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Lagos begins workers’ village, gives contractors weeks to complete Egan-Igando housing project
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Lagos State Government has commenced the construction of a new workers’ village at Ajasa, Command, in the Ipaja area of the state as part of efforts to expand access to affordable housing, while also directing contractors handling the Egan-Igando Housing Scheme to complete the project with

item.title: NIMC Partners MDAs To Expand Digital Identity, NIN Adoption Nationwide
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) has strengthened partnerships with key ministries to accelerate the implementation of the new NIMC Act 2026, expand National Identification Number (NIN) enrolment and deepen the use of digital identity across government programmes and the private se

item.title: World Cup 2026: It’s A Special Game –Haaland Speaks Ahead Norway Vs England
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Norway striker Erling Haaland has claimed that all the pressure is on England ahead of their FIFA World Cup 2026 quarter-final tie in Miami. The Norwegian striker dubbed Thomas Tuchel’s squad one of the clear favourites to win the tournament. Read Also:World Cup 2026: Why Kane Will Outshine Ha

item.title: Education Ministry, NIMC To Link 80 Million Learners With NIN
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Federal Ministry of Education and the National Identity Management Commission (NIMC) have begun integrating the National Learners’ Identification Number (NLIN) with the National Identification Number (NIN) in a move expected to provide credible data for education planning and service deliv

item.title: Driver remanded for allegedly stabbing couple over N75,000 beer bill in Lagos
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The prosecution alleged that Olajide and his accomplices, who are still at large, organised a mini birthday celebration at the bar where they consumed beers and other drinks valued at N75,000. Trouble reportedly started when the couple requested payment for the drinks.

item.title: NASS sets up conference committee on state police
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Rt. Hon. Benjamin Kalu, on Thursday said the National Assembly had set up a conference committee on state police. The Deputy Speaker explained that the conference committee was set up on Wednesday in Abuja. The committee comprises lawmakers from bo

item.title: Google will now tell you if an ad was made with AI
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: You can see if ads on Google Search, Google Discover, and YouTube were made or edited using AI from a new section in Google's "My Ad Center," as reported earlier by TechCrunch. The update, announced on Thursday, adds a "created or edited with AI" label under the "how this ad was made" tab. Users can

item.title: [VIDEO] Technology Can Enhance Policing, Can’t Replace Public Trust — IGP Disu
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: IGP Tunju Disu said that the benefits of technological advancement should not be limited to a few technologically advanced nations. The post [VIDEO] Technology Can Enhance Policing, Can’t Replace Public Trust — IGP Disu appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Oyo police arrest two suspected ritualists over alleged possession of human skull
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Oyo Police arrest two suspected ritualists over alleged possession of a human skull during an intelligence-led operation in Ijio. The post Oyo police arrest two suspected ritualists over alleged possession of human skull appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NLO: Enyimba Stadium to host South-South,South-East promotion playoffs
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nationwide League One, NLO, has confirmed the Enyimba International Stadium, Aba as hosts for the 2026 Promotion playoffs for the South-South and South-East. The playoffs will run from Wednesday July 29th to Sunday August 2nd. The NLO Chief Operating Officer, Dr. Olushola Ogunnowo, disclosed thi

item.title: NASCO Town appeals High Court judgment on Lagos land, warns against transactions pending appeal
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: According to the statement, anyone transacting with parties other than NASCO over the land would do so at their own risk and subject to the outcome of the appellate proceedings.

item.title: PFIPC scandal: ADC calls for independent panel of inquiry
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: African Democratic Congress, ADC, on Thursday called for the constitution of an independent panel of inquiry into the activities of the Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council, PFIPC, an alleged fake agency headed by Adeniyi Adeyemi. The post PFIPC scandal: ADC calls for independent pane

item.description: Carney and Saudi Crown Prince Bin Salman strengthen partnerships after years of strained relations under Trudeau.

item.description: President Bola Tinubu on Thursday said the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory, FCT, Nyesom Wike’s efforts of transforming Abuja into a capital city that all Nigerians would be proud of is in line with his vision to build a nation that works through purposeful leadership. This was as Tin

item.description: Deji Elumoye in Abuja The Federal Government will deepen engagement with governors and emergency responders on the adoption of 112 as the national emergency number across the country.This was the

item.title: State police could be Hiroshima Nigerians are not expecting – Defence Minister, Musa
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Minister of Defence, Christopher Musa, has said state police could be the Hiroshima Nigerians are not expecting if not well planned. Musa, however, noted that state police is a good thing if properly planned and executed. Speaking during an Arise Town hall meeting in Abuja on Thursday, Musa said

item.title: South African protesters intimidate migrants as aid group issues warning
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Doctors Without Borders (MSF) warns of growing humanitarian needs as tens of thousands of African migrants displaced.

item.title: Why the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve matters amid US-Iran tensions
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: US oil reserves hit lowest levels since 1983 amid growing tensions with Iran and global supply concerns.

item.title: Police Arrest Two Over Suspected Human Skull In Oyo
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Oyo State Police Command has arrested two suspected ritualists over the unlawful possession of an item believed to be a human skull in Ijio, following an intelligence-led operation. The post Police Arrest Two Over Suspected Human Skull In Oyo appeared first on Trending News.

item.title: Xenophobia: Another batch of stranded Nigerians arrives Lagos from South Africa
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Another batch of stranded Nigerians willing to return from South Africa has arrived at the Murtala Muhammed International Airport, Lagos, aboard an Air Peace evacuation flight. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs disclosed this on Thursday, saying the flight conveyed 284 passengers, comprising 272 adult

item.title: U-17 WWCQ: Flamingos Storm Lomé for Benin Rematch
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Nigeria’s Flamingos have arrived in Lomé, Togo, ahead of Saturday’s decisive 2026 FIFA U-17 Women’s World Cup final qualifying round second-leg clash against Benin Republic’s Young Amazons. The players and officials landed safely at the Gnassingbé Eyadéma International Airpor

item.title: Two more men plead guilty in US for deadly 2021 human-smuggling truck crash
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Five Guatemalan nationals have now plead guilty in the migrant smuggling case linked to the deaths of 55 migrants.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) has unveiled plans to launch a $500 million Agricultural Investment Fund on July 15 to reposition the Niger Delta as a major agricultural hub in Nigeria. Speaking at a press conference at the Presidential Villa on Thursday, the Managing Director and Chie

item.title: Senate okays sale of Lafarge Cement to Chinese firm
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Senate has endorsed the sale of Lafarge Cement Plc to Hainan Huaxin Pan-African Investment Company Plc, recommending that the transaction process should be allowed to sail through. But it also recommended that, in finalising the transaction, the parties must comply with due process and “al

item.description: Osun State Governor Ademola Adeleke has raised alarm over alleged plot by the opposition party to compromise voters' register by mopping up Permanent Voters' Cards across the state, calling on the populace not to release their cards to anyone for pecuniary gains. The post Don’t release your PV

item.dc:creator: The Sun

item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: A group of shareholders have urged the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) to reconsider a proposed capital requirement for Financial Holding Companies (HoldCos), warning that it could lead to repeated capital raising, investor fatigue and weaken confidence in the banking sector. The post CBN’s prop

item.title: Quansah banned for two games after Mexico red card
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: England defender Jarell Quansah receives a two-match ban for his red card against Mexico.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Has football lost its soul? From Pele’s “Joga Bonito” to billion-dollar World Cups, the game has changed.

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Former Senate Leader, Senator Ali Ndume, has advised President Bola Tinubu to speak more directly to Nigerians on his administration’s efforts to address insecurity and the country’s economic challenges. The lawmaker, who spoke with State House correspondents after a closed-door meeting

item.title: Kida calls for national support as D’Tigers begin FIBA World Cup qualifiers
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: President of the Nigeria Basketball Federation (NBBF), Engr. Ahmad Musa Kida, has called on Nigerians to rally behind the senior men’s national basketball team, D’Tigers, as they begin their campaign in the FIBA Africa Basketball World Cup Qualifiers. D’Tigers have been drawn in Group E alongside Ca

item.title: Vaping may cause lung, oral cancers, researchers warn
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Researchers have warned that nicotine-based e-cigarettes are likely to cause lung and oral cancers, raising fresh concerns over the safety of vaping and challenging claims that it is a harmless alternative to smoking. The findings, published in the journal Carcinogenesis, are based on a major review

item.title: Norway's Haaland calls for media pressure on England before quarter-final
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Norway striker Erling Haaland calls on the media to "put every single pressure" on England before the two sides' quarter-final on Saturday.

item.title: New ‘Ex-Elon’ ETFs let you avoid SpaceX and Tesla — but are they just a gimmick?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: One expert doubts that new ETFs from Subversive will catch on in a meaningful way.

item.title: Canal+ CEO says MultiChoice now fully integrated into Global Media Group
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: "MultiChoice is now a full subsidiary of a truly international media group operating in 70 countries. The group was founded in France, is listed in London and Johannesburg, and has a strong African presence with operations in more than 45 countries," Mignot said.

item.title: Nigeria Ranks First In Africa On Latest Global AI Index
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigeria has emerged as the leading African country in the second edition of the Global Index on Responsible Artificial Intelligence AI (GIRAI), ranking 38th globally and 1st in Africa with an overall index score of 45.93. This was disclosed in a statement issued on Thursday by Isime Esene, media aid

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigeria Police Force (NPF) has responded to a trending video in which a Nepalese businessman accused police officers of complicity in a scheme that allegedly defrauded him of ₦1.6 billion. The Force spokesman, a Chief Superintendent of Police (CSP), Mr Anietie Iniedu, said that although no forma

item.title: Court remands lecturer who accused Katsina govt of sponsoring bandits to Hajj
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A Chief Magistrates' Court in Katsina has ordered the police to remand Dr Bashir Kurfi, a lecturer who accused the Katsina State Government of The post Court remands lecturer who accused Katsina govt of sponsoring bandits to Hajj appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Natasha warns against abuse of state police powers
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Senator Natasha Akpoti-Uduaghan warns that state police require safeguards to prevent political abuse and protect citizens’ rights. The post Natasha warns against abuse of state police powers appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Financial literacy just hit a 10-year low — and it is devastating bank accounts.

item.title: Photos: Another batch of Nigerians evacuated from S’Africa arrive Lagos
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Another batch of Nigerians evacuated from South Africa arrived Thursday night on an Air Peace aircraft at the Cargo section of Murtala Muhammed International Airport, Ikeja, Lagos. The post Photos: Another batch of Nigerians evacuated from S’Africa arrive Lagos appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

item.title: Tinubu gets briefing from security chiefs
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Tinubu receives fresh security briefings from military and intelligence chiefs during a high-level meeting in Abuja. The post Tinubu gets briefing from security chiefs appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Senate clears Lafarge acquisition by Chinese investor, says Nigerians’ equity will remain protected
item.dc:creator: BusinessDay
item.description: The Senate on Thursday approved the proposed acquisition of Lafarge Africa Plc by Hainan Huaxin Pan-African Investment Company Plc, assuring read more Senate clears Lafarge acquisition by Chinese investor, says Nigerians’ equity will remain protected

item.title: Africa Moves to Capture Trillions in Carbon Finance, Trains Diplomats for Global Climate Market Negotiations
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: *AU, ACBF equip over 80 ambassadors, senior diplomats as continent pushes for bigger share of carbon trading under Paris agreement Michael Olugbode in Abuja The African Union (AU) and the

item.title: Trust, Sustainability Driving Nigeria’s E-commerce Boom, DHL Report Reveals
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: Nigeria’s e-commerce landscape is undergoing a profound transformation, with trust in delivery services and the rapid embrace of second-hand online trading emerging as two of the strongest forces reshaping consumer
